Eliot and Sir Thomas More, he won the Somerset Maugham Award and two Whitbread Awards. He is noted for the volume of work he has produced, the range of styles therein, his skill at assuming different voices and the depth of his research.\", \"He was elected a fellow of the Royal Society of Literature in 1984 and appointed a Commander of the Order of the British Empire in 2003.\", \"Bio from Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia.\"]}", "categories": "[\"Books\", \"Literature & Fiction\", \"History & Criticism\"]"}, {"book_id": "bookid_9", "title": "Reunion: The Children of Lauderdale Park", "subtitle": "Paperback \u2013 September 25, 2019", "author": "None", "categories": "[\"Books\", \"Literature & Fiction\", \"Genre Fiction\"]"}, {"book_id": "bookid_13", "title": "Girl Made of Glass", "subtitle": "Paperback \u2013 January 24, 2023", "author": "{\"avatar\": \"https://m.media-amazon.com/images/S/amzn-author-media-prod/5av23i12r4p62nfrr8koq0blld._SY600_.jpg\", \"name\": \"Shelby Leigh\", \"about\": [\"Shelby Leigh is a mental health poet writing about self-love, insecurities, anxiety, relationships and more. Her third poetry book, girl made of glass, released in 2023 with Central Avenue Publishing. The new edition was released by Gallery Books in July 2022.\", \"In addition to writing, Shelby runs an online poetry community with workshops and edits poetry books.\", \"Connect with her on Instagram and TikTok @shelbyleighpoetry, Twitter @shelbyleighpoet, and on her website www.shelbyleigh.co.\"]}", "categories": "[\"Books\", \"Literature & Fiction\", \"Poetry\"]"}, {"book_id": "bookid_30", "title": "Sugary Sweets (A Taste of Love Series)", "subtitle": "Paperback \u2013 August 18, 2016", "author": "{\"avatar\": \"https://m.media-amazon.com/images/S/amzn-author-media-prod/i6g5m3i4a99kmtvj00g6v6h55o._SY600_.jpg\", \"name\": \"A.M. args.query: SELECT book_id, title, subtitle, author, categories, details FROM books_info WHERE categories ILIKE '%Literature & Fiction%' AND (details ILIKE '%English%' OR details ILIKE '%Language%English%' OR details ILIKE '%"Language"%' OR details ILIKE '%"language"%');

exec_args.sql: SELECT book_id, title, subtitle, author, categories, details FROM books_info WHERE categories ILIKE '%Literature & Fiction%' AND (details ILIKE '%English%' OR details ILIKE '%Language%English%' OR details ILIKE '%"Language"%' OR details ILIKE '%"language"%');

args.code:
import json, pandas as pd

# load perfect-rating purchase_ids
perfect = pd.DataFrame(var_call_kp7DHOJGVamAbcLBkG9Jbipw)
perfect['purchase_id_num'] = perfect['purchase_id'].str.extract(r'(\d+)$').astype(int)

# load books in Lit & Fiction with English in details
path = var_call_oxBUkgUKHX55KWBLBgB1hAsd
with open(path, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    books = json.load(f)
books_df = pd.DataFrame(books)
books_df['book_id_num'] = books_df['book_id'].str.extract(r'(\d+)$').astype(int)

# fuzzy join via numeric suffix
merged = books_df.merge(perfect, left_on='book_id_num', right_on='purchase_id_num', how='inner')

# derive author name if JSON
import ast

def author_name(a):
    if a is None:
        return None
    if isinstance(a, str):
        s=a
        if s=='None':
            return None
        s_strip=s.strip()
        if s_strip.startswith('{') and 'name' in s_strip:
            try:
                d=json.loads(s_strip)
                return d.get('name')
            except Exception:
                pass
    return a

merged['author_name'] = merged['author'].apply(author_name)

out = merged[['title','subtitle','author_name','book_id','avg_rating','review_count']].sort_values(['title','book_id']).to_dict(orient='records')
print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(out, ensure_ascii=False))

# load books in Lit & Fiction with English in details

with open(path, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    books = json.load(f)
books_df = pd.DataFrame(books)
books_df['book_id_num'] = books_df['book_id'].str.extract(r'(\d+)$').astype(int)

# fuzzy join via numeric suffix
merged = books_df.merge(perfect, left_on='book_id_num', right_on='purchase_id_num', how='inner')

# derive author name if JSON
import ast

def author_name(a):
    if a is None:
        return None
    if isinstance(a, str):
        s=a
        if s=='None':
            return None
        s_strip=s.strip()
        if s_strip.startswith('{') and 'name' in s_strip:
            try:
                d=json.loads(s_strip)
                return d.get('name')
            except Exception:
                pass
    return a

merged['author_name'] = merged['author'].apply(author_name)

out = merged[['title','subtitle','author_name','book_id','avg_rating','review_count']].sort_values(['title','book_id']).to_dict(orient='records')
print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(out, ensure_ascii=False))
